explanation: This command matches the Sigma rule by using gpg.exe with the decryption flag (-d) and a passphrase. It serves as a baseline for testing detection.
Quotes are inserted around the passphrase to test parsing behavior while maintaining the same functionality.
The order of the flags is changed to test if the detection is sensitive to argument ordering.
Whitespace is minimized to test if the detection relies on spacing between command-line arguments.
The passphrase is passed as an argument without a space to test if the detection is sensitive to spacing or formatting.
This is a standard command that matches the Sigma rule and serves as a reference for the original test case.
